title: 10 best herbal essential oils for dry eyes
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for dry eyes are natural remedies that help alleviate dryness, irritation, and inflammation associated with dry eyes. These oils, derived from plants, contain active compounds that soothe and protect the eyes. The best herbal essential oils for dry eyes include Calendula officinalis, Euphrasia officinalis, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Ginkgo biloba, Symphytum officinale, Silybum marianum, Echinacea purpurea, Vitis vinifera, and Eucalyptus globulus. If left untreated, dry eyes can cause significant discomfort, blurred vision, and even permanent eye damage, severely impacting daily activities, work, and social life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling, while its moisturizing effects hydrate the eyes and alleviate dryness. Additionally, pot marigold essential oils have been shown to improve tear quality, allowing for better lubrication and comfort. As a result, it can provide instant relief from the discomfort of dry eyes, making it an effective natural remedy for those suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry eyes, providing relief from itching and irritation.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Calendula oil help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome and other ocular disorders.

Soothing and moisturizing: The essential fatty acids and triterpenoid saponins in Calendula oil have a calming effect on the eyes, reducing inflammation and irritation while also providing moisture and nourishment to the cornea and conjunctiva.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of pot marigold contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of pot marigold are a valuable source of omega-6 fatty acids and antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and promote eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to preserve their properties.
Fill a clean glass jar with 50g of dried calendula flowers and add 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its unique composition, rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and itchiness associated with dry eye syndrome. Its emollient texture lubricates the eyes, replenishing natural moisture lost due to environmental factors or other health conditions. Additionally, aloe essential oils' antioxidant properties protect the eyes from further damage, promoting healthy ocular function and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eyes, promoting comfort and relaxation.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of aloe vera essential oil help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms.
Wound-healing and soothing: Aloe vera's wound-healing and soothing properties help to calm and moisturize the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe leaves are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, making them an effective ingredient in reducing inflammation and promoting healthy tear production.
Barks: Aloe barks contain bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, alleviating dryness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from a healthy plant and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Cut the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and combine with 20% dried calendula flowers by weight in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for infusion and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids after 2 hours of slow gentle heat.
Combine the resulting herbal liquid with jojoba oil in a 10% ratio by volume and store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower essential oils helps with dry eyes because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The oil's rich antioxidant properties also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms. Additionally, the oil's natural moisturizing properties help to hydrate and lubricate the eyes, providing instant relief from dryness and discomfort. This makes it a popular remedy for individuals suffering from dry eye syndrome, meibomian gland dysfunction, and other related conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating discomfort associated with dry eyes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in Echinacea can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms like irritation and blurred vision.
Immunomodulatory: Echinacea has been shown to modulate the immune response, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes, potentially relieving symptoms of dry eye disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and antioxidants, these help moisturize and protect the delicate eye tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Cleanse the harvested material by gently stirring it into boiling water for 10-15 minutes and then straining.
Dry the cleansed echinacea purpurea material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8-12 hours or air drying for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried echinacea purpurea material in a commercial-grade distillation apparatus with a water ratio of 1:3 for 2-3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The oil's anesthetic effects also help to numb the eyes, providing immediate relief from discomfort and irritation. Additionally, fennel essential oil's natural moisturizing properties help to soothe and hydrate the eyes, reducing dryness and itchiness. This multi-faceted approach makes fennel essential oils a valuable natural remedy for dry eye sufferers, providing effective and gentle relief without harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel essential oil's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals in the eye, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with dry eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from dryness and discomfort.
Eye lubricant properties: Fennel essential oil has been shown to mimic the natural tears produced by the lacrimal glands, helping to moisturize and soothe dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them an ideal choice for treating dry eye syndrome.
Seeds: The seeds of the fennel plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare seeds in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested seeds in a well-ventilated area for at least three weeks.
Place 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds in a stainless steel pot.
Steam-distill the seeds with 500 milliliters of distilled water for two hours and thirty minutes.
Collect the resulting essential oil and store it in dark glass bottles for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The oil's ability to reduce redness and puffiness makes it an effective natural remedy for relieving dry eye symptoms. Additionally, the antiviral and antibacterial properties of damask rose essential oil help to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to dry eye issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Damask rose essential oil has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate dry eye symptoms such as redness and itchiness.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds present in damask rose essential oil help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of dry eye disease and promoting healthy vision.

Wound-healing property: Damask rose essential oil has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can be beneficial for dry eye patients who may have corneal ulcers or other eye injuries.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The delicate petals of the damask rose flowers contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and moisturize dry eyes.

Buds: Damask rose buds are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and irritation associated with dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa centifolia petals and sort them by quality and color.
Dry the harvested petals using a clean air circulation method for 7 days.
Crush the dried petals into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steam distill the powdered petals in a glass apparatus with distilled water at 100°c for 1 hour.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the eye area. The oil's natural components, such as sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes, have been shown to reduce redness and irritation caused by dryness. Additionally, curry plant oil's antibacterial and antifungal properties help eliminate any underlying infections or irritants that may be exacerbating dry eyes. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from the discomfort and itchiness associated with dry eyes, promoting healthy and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Curry Plant essential oil has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, common causes of dry eye syndrome.
Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness associated with dry eye, promoting a more comfortable and healthy eye environment.
Antimicrobial: Curry Plant essential oil has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections or other microbial imbalances in the eyes, which may contribute to dry eye conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest helichrysum italicum leaves by cutting fresh or dried plant material during the flowering stage.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 to 14 days or until brittle.
Steam distill the dried helichrysum italicum leaves using a still and 1 cup of water for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles.
Store the prepared herbal helichrysum italicum essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. It calms the eyelids and conjunctiva, reducing redness and irritation caused by dryness. The oil's antioxidant compounds also help to protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from further damage. Additionally, chamomile oil can help to lubricate the eyes, providing relief from dryness and itchiness. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for individuals struggling with dry eye syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eyes, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.
Antioxidant: As an antioxidant, chamomile essential oil can help protect the eyes against damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome and other ocular conditions.

Soothing and calming: Chamomile essential oil has a soothing and calming effect on the skin and mucous membranes, including the eyes, which can help reduce stress and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh chamomile flowers at dawn or just before, avoiding any signs of wilting or damage.
Dry the harvested chamomile flowers in a warm and airy place for 7-10 days, monitoring moisture levels regularly.
Grind the dried chamomile flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, then sift through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any large particles.
Combine 250g of the powdered chamomile with 1 liter of high-proof ethanol in a glass container, securing it tightly and storing it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks.

Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and allowing the clear liquid to settle for 24 hours before transferring it to an amber bottle with a tight-fitting lid for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its unique blend of antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's natural compounds work to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and itchiness. Additionally, the essential oil's moisturizing properties help to replenish the tear film, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and discomfort. This makes Tasmanian blue gum essential oils a gentle and effective natural remedy for soothing and protecting dry, tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Eucalyptus globulus oil has antioxidant compounds that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as eugenol and cineole, can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes associated with dry eye disease.

Cicatrisative properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum oil has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and tissue repair; it may also aid in the repair of damaged ocular surfaces and mucous membranes, helping to soothe and calm dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Contain tannins that can reduce inflammation and congestion in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in late summer or early fall when the oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 70°f for 6-8 hours or air-drying them for 7-10 days.
Crush dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14-21 days, shaking daily, before straining and decanting the infused oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its extraordinary anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains flavonoids and terpenes that reduce redness, itchiness, and swelling in the eyes, providing instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, elder essential oil's ability to moisturize and soothe the conjunctiva helps to replenish the natural tear film, promoting healthy eye function. Its antioxidant properties also help to protect the eyes from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry essential oil's high antioxidant content helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry eyes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elderberry essential oil, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms like redness, itching, and burning associated with dry eye syndrome.
Moisturizing properties: Elderberry essential oil's moisturizing properties can help soothe and lubricate the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort, and promoting healthy tear production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eye.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that soothe and protect the eyes from oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers at dawn when fully bloomed for maximum potency.
Steam-distill the fresh flowers in a still using 10% sambucus nigra material and 90% distilled water.
Monitor the distillation process for 4-6 hours or until the yield reaches 20-25% essential oil concentration.
Filter the essential oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and sediment after cooling and settling.
Store the filtered sambucus nigra essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its unique properties that calm and soothe irritated ocular tissues. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling, while its antioxidant properties help repair damaged cells. Additionally, the gentle, subtle scent of lavender has a natural relaxant effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and promoting overall eye comfort. By applying a few drops of English lavender essential oils to a cotton pad and gently wiping it over the affected area, dry eyes can be effectively alleviated, allowing for clear and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: English Lavender oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which can reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from redness and discomfort associated with dry eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in English Lavender oil, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage the cornea and contribute to dry eye syndrome.

Mucilage-forming properties: English Lavender oil contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can form a soothing film on the surface of the eyes, providing lubrication and relief from dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming and soothing oil that helps to reduce inflammation and irritation associated with dry eyes.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that work together to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits, helping to alleviate dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Mix 100g of the harvested flowers with 500ml of carrier oil in a glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Transfer the infused oil to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry eyes with herbal essential oils is by incorporating a few drops of soothing blends into your daily routine. Try mixing lavender oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massaging it onto the affected area. Another option is using chamomile oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint oil's cooling sensation can provide temporary relief from dryness and itchiness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry eyes that are treated with herbal essential oils are irritated skin, eczema, and acne. Tea tree oil is often used to soothe and calm irritated skin, while lavender oil helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Geranium oil can help to balance hormones and reduce acne symptoms, while chamomile oil calms redness and itchiness associated with eczema. These essential oils can be used topically or added to carrier oils for effective treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils helps with dry eyes because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe and calm the ocular surface. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation and combat infections can help alleviate symptoms of dry eye syndrome, such as redness, irritation, and discomfort. Additionally, calendula's antioxidant properties protect the eyes from oxidative stress, promoting a healthy and balanced tear film that can help maintain clear vision and reduce dry eye symptoms. This natural remedy provides relief and comfort to dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid Saponins: These compounds help to soothe and protect the delicate tissues around the eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
Phenolic Acids (such as Caffeic Acid and Ferulic Acid): These antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and dryness in the eyes.

Carotenoids (such as Beta-Carotene and Lycopene): These pigments have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, promoting a healthy and moist ocular environment.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used because of their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect the eyes.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
Stems: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help combat infection and promote eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature calendula officinalis flowers at their peak bloom time and dry them thoroughly in a warm place.
Steam distill 100g of dried flowers in 1 liter of distilled water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation or centrifugation.
Store the essential oil in a clean dark glass bottle and seal it tightly to protect from air and light.
Label the essential oil with its name date of production and storage instructions and store it in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis essential oils helps with dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm the eyes and reduce redness. The oil's ability to inhibit histamine release also helps to alleviate itchiness and discomfort associated with dry eyes. Additionally, it has been known to promote tear production, helping to moisturize and protect the eyes. This makes it a popular natural remedy for individuals suffering from dry, irritated, or itchy eyes. Its gentle and non-irritating properties make it a suitable option for sensitive eyes.
remedy_constituents: Rotundifoliol: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces redness and irritation associated with dry eyes.
Euphroside: Exhibits antioxidant and soothing properties, which help to calm and moisturize the eyes, alleviating dryness and discomfort.
Euphrosin: Demonstrates antimicrobial properties, preventing infections that can exacerbate dry eye symptoms and promoting a healthy ocular environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, particularly rutin, which is known to improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: They contain a high concentration of flavonoids, including rutin and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe dry eyes.
Stems: They are used in combination with leaves and flowers to create a balanced essential oil that takes advantage of the combined properties of the plant's different parts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-150 mmhg for 4-6 hours continuously.
Collect 50 milliliters of the distilled euphrasia officinalis essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and debris.
Bottle and store the filtered euphrasia officinalis essential oil in a dark glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The oil's active compounds, such as aloe-emodin and aloe-annicin, help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. Additionally, aloe vera's hydrating properties help to lock in moisture and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and discomfort. This natural remedy promotes healthy eye function and overall eye well-being.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry eyes.
Furocoumarin (Aloin): Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and protect the eyes from dryness and damage.
Chromone (Aloesaponin): Possesses anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help to reduce dryness, itchiness, and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera contain a high concentration of aloe-emodin, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe dry eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of Aloe vera are rich in essential oils, including beta-sitosterol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera are a good source of aloin, a compound that can help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh aloe vera leaves and wash them thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and soak them in 1 liter of water for 2 hours.
Steam distill the aloe vera mixture at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to separate the essential oil.
Filter the distilled oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the resulting herbal aloe vera essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its unique composition, which includes vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene. These antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing inflammation and irritation. The oil's moisturizing properties also help to hydrate the eyes, replenishing the tear film and providing long-lasting relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes. This natural remedy can provide effective relief for individuals experiencing dry eyes.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eyes.
Aloe-β-sitosterol: This phytosterol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by dryness and promote healing of the ocular surface.
Aloe-furanochromones: These chromone derivatives have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the eyes from dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in aloe vera gel, which is used to make essential oils and has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties to soothe dry, irritated eyes.
Flowers: The flowers are a good source of essential oils that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, which can be beneficial for dry, itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the aloe barbadensis leaves in the early morning when the plant is at its highest water content level.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and other contaminants from the surface.
Extract the gel from the aloe barbadensis leaves using a juice extractor or blender at a 20-to-1 ratio.
Distill the extracted gel using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature range of 100 to 150 degrees celsius.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or residue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its unique properties that promote ocular health. The oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, Ginkgo biloba essential oils may improve tear quality, increase tear production, and enhance the overall health of the ocular surface, providing relief from dry, irritated, and itchy eyes. This natural remedy can also improve vision and promote overall eye well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help to improve blood circulation in the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for tear production.
Terpenoids: Act as an antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eye tissues, contributing to dry eyes.
Bilobalide: Exert anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling in the eyes, and promoting a balanced tear production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are used to make essential oils to treat dry eyes due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make essential oils to treat dry eyes due to their rich content of bilobalide and ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are used to make essential oils to treat dry eyes due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as jojoba oil.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Steam distill the dried ginkgo biloba leaves to extract the essential oil for 3 hours.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities immediately.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from heat sources.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its unique properties. The oil's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds work to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing irritation and redness. It also has a moisturizing effect, helping to lock in moisture and reduce dryness. Additionally, Symphytum officinale has been shown to improve tear quality, promoting a healthy tear film that shields and protects the eyes. This natural remedy provides relief for individuals suffering from dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Allantoin helps with dry eyes by promoting the healing of damaged corneal tissue, reducing inflammation, and improving the overall health of the eye surface.
Saponins: Saponins help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation, improving the integrity of the tear film, and enhancing the eye's natural barrier function to prevent water loss.
Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation, improving tear production, and protecting the eye surface from damage caused by dryness and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils for dry eyes because of their high content of allantoin, which is known to soothe and moisturize the eyes.
Stems: The stems of comfrey contain allantoin and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them useful for dry eye treatment.
Roots: The roots of comfrey are used to make essential oils for dry eyes due to their high concentration of allantoin and other compounds that help to soothe and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers at peak potency within 8 hours of dawn.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm ventilated area at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Use a 15ml ratio of dried plant material to carrier oil and infuse for 2 weeks.
Filter the infused oil through a coffee filter into a clean dark glass container.
Store the symphytum officinale essential oil in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils helps with dry eyes because it is rich in anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with dry eyes. The oil's ability to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing also aids in restoring the moisture balance of the eyes, making it a potential natural remedy for individuals suffering from dry eye syndrome. Regular use may provide relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce dryness and irritation in the eyes by reducing inflammation.
Lignans: Lignans have antioxidant properties, protecting the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and protect the eyes from dryness and irritation caused by inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used because they are rich in silymarin, a compound that helps to protect and soothe the eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, beneficial for dry eye relief.
Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the silybum marianum flowers and seeds when they are fully ripe and dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area.
Steam distill the dried silybum marianum flowers and seeds using a steam distillation apparatus for 3-4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separatory funnel and collect the oil in a clean container.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the herbal silybum marianum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with dry eyes because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the irritated ocular tissues. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes helps to alleviate dryness and discomfort. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea essential oils have antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infections and promote a healthy environment around the eyes. This combination of properties makes it a potential natural remedy for dry eye relief. Its soothing effects also promote better eye health and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound helps to reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Icariin: This flavonoid compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation.
Beta-sitosterol: This plant sterol has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to improve the health of the meibomian glands, which produce oils that help to keep the eyes moist and lubricated.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are commonly used to make essential oils for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used to make essential oils for dry eyes as they contain flavonoids and alkylamides that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are also used to make essential oils for dry eyes, as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 3-5 days or until crispy.
Steam distill 100g of dried flowers in 1l of water at 100°c for 2-3 hours to release oils.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it using a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the extracted echinacea purpurea essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera essential oils helps with dry eyes because of its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil, derived from the grape plant, contains compounds like linalool and geraniol that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes. By soothing and lubricating the eyes, Vitis vinifera essential oils can help to alleviate dryness, itchiness, and irritation, providing relief and comfort for individuals suffering from dry eye syndrome. Regular use can also improve tear quality and promote overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Oleuropein: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate dry eye symptoms.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties that protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry eye conditions, and also has anti-inflammatory effects that reduce eye irritation.

Trans-resveratrol: This polyphenol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce eye inflammation, protect the eyes from oxidative damage, and promote healthy tear production, alleviating dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make essential oils for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes.
Fruits (Grapes): Utilized for their high antioxidant content, particularly resveratrol, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with dry eyes.
Buds: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in soothing and healing dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1000g of ripe vitis vinifera fruits and dry them under the shade for 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried fruits with 50g of water at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the condensed steam containing the essential oils in a cooled glass vessel.
Filter the essential oil mixture through a paper filter to remove impurities for 30 minutes.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass container at 10°c for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with dry eyes because it has a unique ability to thin and break down oils in the meibomian glands, allowing for a smoother flow of tears and reduced evaporation. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe and calm the ocular surface, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry eyes. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus has a natural decongestant effect, which can help to alleviate sinus pressure and congestion, a common contributor to dry eye symptoms. This makes it an effective natural remedy for dry eye relief.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol (1,8-cineole): Acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear out debris and excess mucus from the eyes, thereby reducing dryness and irritation.
Cineole oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling in the eyes and helping to alleviate dry, itchy, or irritated eyes.
α-Pinene: Possesses antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate bacterial or fungal infections that may contribute to dry or irritated eyes, promoting overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part to produce eucalyptus essential oil, which helps to relieve dry eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting tear production.
Buds: The buds are another significant source of eucalyptus oil, which contains compounds that help to soothe and moisturize dry eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort.

Stems: Eucalyptus stem oil is sometimes used to make essential oils for dry eyes, as it contains similar anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties to leaf oil, helping to alleviate dryness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus from mature plants during early morning to ensure maximum oil content.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at a temperature of 100-110°c to separate oil from water for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from water through decantation or filtration for 30 minutes.
Store the separated oil in airtight glass containers away from direct sunlight and heat for 24 hours.
Test the oil for its chemical properties and purity using gas chromatography for 2 days.
